title: Systems and methods for blockchains with serial proof of work
abstract: A system and method for blockchains with serial proof of work includes a memory storing a blockchain, and a processor coupled to the memory. The processor is configured to receive a miner identifier, receive a block of data for inclusion in a new block of the blockchain, determine an initial nonce based on the miner identifier, hash a combination of the block of data and the initial nonce to create a hashed value, iteratively determine an updated nonce based on the hashed value and update the hashed value by hashing the updated nonce until the updated hashed value satisfies a proof of work criteria, create the new block based on the block of data, the miner identifier, and the updated hashed value that satisfies the proof of work criteria, and share the new block with one or more other computing devices hosting the blockchain.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A computing device comprising: a memory storing a blockchain; and a processor coupled to the memory, the processor being configured to: receive a miner identifier; receive a block of data for inclusion in a new block of the blockchain; determine an initial nonce based on the miner identifier; hash a combination of the block of data and the initial nonce to create a hashed value; iteratively determine an updated nonce based on the hashed value and update the hashed value by hashing the updated nonce until the updated hashed value satisfies a proof of work criteria; and create the new block based on the block of data, the miner identifier, and the updated hashed value that satisfies the proof of work criteria; and share the new block with one or more other computing devices hosting the blockchain. 2. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to add the new block to the blockchain based on reaching a consensus with the one or more other computing devices that the new block should be added to the blockchain. 3. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the updated nonce is determined by combining the updated hashed value from a previous iteration and a deterministic value. 4. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the proof of work criteria is a predetermined number of leading zeros in the updated hashed value. 5. The computing device of claim 1, wherein SHA512 is used to hash the updated nonce. 6. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to: count a number of times a hashed value is determined; and 

 every predetermined number of times a hashed value is determined, save interim results from the hashing. 7. The computing device of claim 6, wherein the interim results from the hashing is an updated hashed value that does not meet the proof of work criteria. 8. The computing device of claim 7, wherein the interim results further include the updated nonce determined from the updated hashed value that does not meet the proof of work criteria. 9. The computing device of claim 6, wherein the new block comprises: the block of data; the miner identifier; each of the interim results; a final nonce value that when hashed results in the updated hashed value that satisfies the proof of work criteria; and the updated hashed value that satisfies the proof of work criteria. 10. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the miner identifier is a public key assigned to the computing device. 11. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the miner identifier includes a first miner identifier assigned to the computing device and a second miner identifier assigned to a host miner for the computing device. 12. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the computing device is further configured to: receive a first block from another computing device from the one or more other computing devices; randomly select a subset of interim results included in the first block; validate the subset of interim results; and report results of the validation to each of the one or more other computing devices; and add the first block to the blockchain based on reaching a consensus with the one or more other computing devices that the new block should be added to the blockchain. 13. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the computing device and the one or more other computing devices form a peer-to-peer network hosting the blockchain. 14. The computing device of claim 1, wherein a first size of the block of data is different from a second size of a block of data previously added to the blockchain. 15. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the block of data includes one or more encrypted items. 16. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the block of data conforms to one or more rules. 17. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the block of data includes a script or executable code. 18. The computing device of claim 1, wherein iteratively determining the updated nonce is performed when the processor is otherwise idle. 19. A method comprising: receiving, by a computing device, a miner identifier; receiving, by the computing device, a block of data for inclusion in a new block of a blockchain; determining, by the computing device, an initial nonce based on the miner identifier; hashing, by the computing device, a combination of the block of data and the initial nonce to create a hashed value; iteratively determining, by the computing device, an updated nonce based on the hashed value and update the hashed value by hashing the updated nonce until the updated hashed value satisfies a proof of work criteria; creating, by the computing device, the new block based on the block of data, the miner identifier, and the updated hashed value that satisfies the proof of work criteria; and sharing, by the computing device, the new block with one or more other computing devices hosting the blockchain. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising, adding, by the computing device, the new block to the blockchain based on reaching a consensus with the one or more other computing devices that the new block should be added to the blockchain. 21. The method of claim 19, determining the updated nonce comprises combining the updated hashed value from a previous iteration and a deterministic value. 22. The method of claim 19, further comprising: counting, by the computing device, a number of times a hashed value is determined; and every predetermined number of times a hashed value is determined, saving, by the computing device, interim results from the hashing. 23. A non-transitory computer-readable medium comprising a plurality of machine-readable instructions which when executed by one or more processors associated with a computing device are adapted to cause the one or more processors to perform a method comprising: receiving a miner identifier; receiving a block of data for inclusion in a new block of a blockchain; determining an initial nonce based on the miner identifier; hashing a combination of the block of data and the initial nonce to create a hashed value; iteratively determining an updated nonce based on the hashed value and update the hashed value by hashing the updated nonce until the updated hashed value satisfies a proof of work criteria; creating the new block based on the block of data, the miner identifier, and the updated hashed value that satisfies the proof of work criteria; and sharing the new block with one or more other computing devices hosting the blockchain. 24. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 23, wherein the method further comprises: counting a number of times a hashed value is determined; and every predetermined number of times a hashed value is determined, saving interim results from the hashing. 25. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 23, wherein the plurality of machine-readable instructions are part of another application. 26. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 25, wherein the another application includes a user interface displaying information indicating progress toward determining the updated hashed value that satisfies the proof of work criteria. 27. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 25, wherein the another application performs the method in response to activity within the another application. 28. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 25, wherein the another application provides a reward in response to determining the updated hashed value that satisfies the proof of work criteria. 29. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 25, wherein the another application is hosted on a server.
